description: Expand ccb_codereview from 3 tasks (2 at ceiling) to 8 tasks with harder multi-file defects and security-adjacent bugs. Uses existing hybrid F1+fix scoring pattern.

userStories.title: Create cr-envoy-001: Multi-file defect review in Envoy C++ filter
userStories.description: As a benchmark developer, I want a code review task with 5-7 injected defects spanning 4+ files in Envoy's HTTP filter chain. At least 2 defects should require cross-file reasoning (e.g., filter A changes response header but filter B assumes old header format).
acceptanceCriteria: benchmarks/ccb_codereview/cr-envoy-001/ has complete task files (task.toml, instruction.md, environment/Dockerfile, environment/inject_defects.sh, tests/test.sh, tests/expected_defects.json, tests/expected_patches/)
inject_defects.sh modifies 4+ files in source/extensions/filters/ or source/common/http/
expected_defects.json has 5-7 defects, at least 2 with cross_file=true
test.sh uses hybrid scoring: 50% detection F1 + 50% fix quality (follow cr-aspnetcore-001 pattern)
Expected baseline score: 0.4-0.6 (verify cross-file defects are hard to find without find_references)
Task registered in configs/selected_benchmark_tasks.json and codereview_2config.sh
userStories.notes: Study existing cr-aspnetcore-001 inject_defects.sh and test.sh for the exact pattern. inject_defects.sh uses sed to introduce bugs. expected_patches/ has one .patch file per defect.

userStories.title: Create cr-vscode-001: Multi-file defect review in VS Code TypeScript
userStories.description: As a benchmark developer, I want a code review task in microsoft/vscode with defects in the extension API or editor component. 5-7 defects spanning 4+ TypeScript files.
acceptanceCriteria: At least 2 cross-file defects (e.g., event handler registered in file A with wrong type expected by listener in file B)
userStories.notes: VS Code src/vs/editor/ and src/vs/workbench/ have deeply interconnected components. Good for cross-file defects.

userStories.title: Create cr-terraform-001: Multi-file defect review in Terraform Go
userStories.description: As a benchmark developer, I want a code review task in hashicorp/terraform with defects in the plan/apply pipeline. 5-7 defects spanning 4+ Go files.
acceptanceCriteria: language=go, 5-7 defects, 4+ files in internal/terraform/ or internal/command/
userStories.notes: Terraform's internal/terraform/ has graph walker, context, eval, plan — many cross-file dependencies.

userStories.title: Create cr-security-001: Security-adjacent review in curl
userStories.description: As a benchmark developer, I want a code review task with security-relevant defects in curl/curl. Defects include: missing input validation, integer overflow, buffer boundary issue, and use-after-free pattern. 4-6 defects with security severity weights.
acceptanceCriteria: expected_defects.json assigns severity=critical to security defects (weighted higher in scoring)
test.sh weights security defects 2x in fix scoring
Expected baseline score: 0.3-0.5 (security bugs are subtle)
userStories.notes: curl's lib/ directory has many security-sensitive functions. Inject realistic security bugs: off-by-one in buffer allocation, missing NULL check before dereference, unchecked return value from malloc.

userStories.title: Create cr-security-002: Security-adjacent review in Kafka Java
userStories.description: As a benchmark developer, I want a security-flavored code review in apache/kafka. Defects include: authentication bypass, unsafe deserialization, missing authorization check. 4-6 defects.
userStories.notes: Kafka's clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/security/ and server/src/main/java/kafka/security/ are good targets. Inject auth bypass, missing ACL check, etc.
